source_ref: Upstream commit affa5a0567a64f79bb4c5aae891889d4af50a72a in groundtruth-kb (docs/architecture/adrs/ADR-ISOLATION-APPLICATION-PLACEMENT-001.md); Agent Red bridge bridge/gtkb-adr-isolation-application-placement-004.md (Codex GO).
full_content:
## Context

GT-KB is an Internal Developer Platform. Adopter applications consume GT-KB's shared infrastructure (specs, work items, bridge protocol, deliberation archive, MemBase, dashboard, hooks, skills). Two filesystem topologies were considered:

- Sibling topology: GT-KB at one path, applications at separate paths (no shared parent). The Phase 9 plan was authored under this topology.
- Hosted topology: applications live as subdirectories of `<gt-kb-root>/applications/`. GT-KB hosts adopter projects.

The hosted topology is chosen.

## Decision

Adopter applications adopting GroundTruth-KB live as named subdirectories under `<gt-kb-root>/applications/<name>/`, where `<gt-kb-root>` is the platform's project root and `<name>` is a valid identifier matching `^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$`. The application's filesystem boundary is a strict descendant of `<gt-kb-root>/applications/`. The platform retains write authority on its own product artifacts; the application has write authority on its own root and any platform-allowed adopter-owned artifacts as defined by the managed artifact registry.

## Failed Approaches

No prior implementation attempts. This is a first-application decision rather than a failure correction. The Phase 9 plan paragraph at lines 93-98 of the GTKB-ISOLATION-009 plan document specified a sibling topology, but it was prospective design (never implemented). The supersession by this ADR is a refinement of the plan's prospective direction, not a recovery from a failed attempt.

## Consequences

### Positive
- IDP semantics: a platform that hosts projects has those projects inside the platform's filesystem boundary. Mirrors how Kubernetes, Backstage, and Heroku-style PaaS organize tenant projects.
- Cross-root portability not required: harness-adjacent infrastructure (`.claude/`, `.codex/`, `groundtruth.db`, `bridge/`, dashboard, hooks, skills) all resolves from project root via `Path(__file__).resolve().parents[N]`. Apps live inside that root; no portability claim.
- Mechanical enforcement: `gt project init <name>` creates `<gt-kb-root>/applications/<name>/` and physically cannot land outside.
- First-migration template: Agent Red establishes the convention; future adopters follow.

### Negative
- Multi-IDP scenarios (one developer machine hosting multiple GT-KB installations with their own applications/ namespaces) are awkward but possible. Each GT-KB root has its own applications/ subtree.
- Adopters who would otherwise prefer their app to live elsewhere on the filesystem (e.g., for company-specific repo organization) lose that flexibility.

### Risks
- Cross-root portability claim might be needed in future (e.g., monorepo separation): mitigated by ADR amendment via successor ADR. Reversible decision.
- Upstream tooling assumes single applications/ namespace: this is a feature, not a bug. If multi-IDP is ever needed, it requires an explicit superseding ADR.

## Alternatives Considered

| Alternative | Why Rejected |
|------------|--------------|
| Sibling topology (per Phase 9 plan paragraph 93-98) | Cross-root portability unvalidated; weaker IDP semantics; the prior plan was prospective, not a tested implementation |
| Per-project drive separation (separate Windows drive for the application) | Cross-drive operations not atomic on Windows for `os.replace`-style moves; adds complexity to `_common.py`; weaker IDP semantics |
| Subdirectory of legacy mixed root (e.g., `<gt-kb-root>/.adopters/<name>/`) | Conflates platform internals (dot-prefixed) with adopter projects which are product surface |

## Related Specs

- Supersedes Phase 9 plan paragraph at lines 93-98 of `independent-progress-assessments/CODEX-INSIGHT-DROPBOX/GTKB-ISOLATION-009-PHASE9-ADOPTER-PACKAGING-AND-VALIDATION-PLAN-2026-04-23.md` (Agent Red repository).
- Bridge thread: `bridge/gtkb-adr-isolation-application-placement-{001,002,003,004}.md` - proposal, NO-GO, REVISED, GO.
- Bridge thread downstream (pending): `bridge/gtkb-isolation-016-phase8-rehearsal-implementation-013.md` - first migration following this convention.

## Upstream Source-of-Truth

The authoritative form of this ADR is the markdown file at `docs/architecture/adrs/ADR-ISOLATION-APPLICATION-PLACEMENT-001.md` in the upstream `groundtruth-kb` repository, committed at upstream commit `affa5a0567a64f79bb4c5aae891889d4af50a72a` on 2026-04-26. The Agent Red KB row reflects the same decision for runtime queries and assertion linkage; the upstream commit hash is the immutable cross-repo reference.
explicit_change_request: Proceed with Option B - Supersede Phase 9 plan: File a formal ADR ADR-ISOLATION-APPLICATION-PLACEMENT-001 capturing the apps-under-GT-KB convention as binding, AND revise the Phase 9 plan to match.
change_reason: Owner-directed Option B (S310 2026-04-26). Adopter-side mirror of upstream ADR at commit affa5a0; per-adopter KB insertion captures the architectural decision for runtime queries.
supersedes: GTKB-ISOLATION-009 Phase 9 plan paragraph at lines 93-98
